subsystem: victory_strategic (Peninsular Sovereignty / peninsular_strain Accord-Turmoil / GD-3 insurgency pipeline / player_agency strategic layer)
core_loop: All factions share one endpoint: hold all 15 territories, Accord≥2, Turmoil≤6, sustained 2 Accountings (GD-1). Four faction toolkits (Treaty/Seizure/Proclamation/Martial Governance; Varfell=military-only) reach Accord≥2 cheaper than conquest (Accord 1). Battle costs MS; held-instability (not battle-occurrence) drives Turmoil/IP. Neglected territory auto-decays to Uncontrolled, triggering GD-3 Insurgency formation, which is itself GD-1 victory-eligible.

decision_points.where: Per-target acquisition method (military vs faction toolkit)
decision_points.options: conquest / Treaty / Seizure / Proclamation / Martial Governance, each with distinct Accord/Turmoil/MS/CB cost
only total-hegemony (15/15) remains live; Partition/co-victory struck by GD-1
3-9 opportunities vs 3-5 scene actions, Conviction vs Duty triage
suppress via GD-2 mandatory response vs tolerate/negotiate
decision_points.note: mechanically real but little authored political texture

north_star.collision_feed: Accord/Turmoil world-state (revolt, insurgency emergence) intersecting faction toolkits and player Convictions/Duties is a solid autonomous-world generator; weaker on producing genuinely different endings.
north_star.verdict: Points at the North Star tactically (rich toolkit/personal-layer choice) but only partially at the strategic-shape level, since GD-1 collapsed 9 distinct endgame shapes into 1.

nq.observation: Four-pressure design (Accord/Turmoil/MS/IP) is a genuine anti-rush, non-dominance device — military conquest structurally punished vs political toolkits.
GD-1 canon text names the track 'Political Stability'; every design doc/sim calls it 'Turmoil' — only an inline sim comment aliases them.
sim/autoload/victory.py correctly hardcodes all-15 threshold uniformly, matching victory_v30/peninsular_strain (the sole-victory-function is sound at the code layer).

threadwork_junctures.evidence: peninsular_strain_v30.md §3.1: MS -1/-2 per battle type on Valorian soil

threadwork_junctures.juncture: Community Weaving (PT mechanic) as Thread operation
threadwork_junctures.evidence: victory_v30_infill.md: 'Community Weaving is a Thread operation... Co-Movement card draw... P-01 auto-effects'

threadwork_junctures.juncture: Insurgency formation (territorial neglect/Uncontrolled) → Gap/Knot substrate event
threadwork_junctures.evidence: insurgency_pipeline_v30.md §4.1 formation trigger cites only adjacency/Uncontrolled status, no Gap/Knot/calamity_radiation citation despite the natural fit

legibility.note: Whose position at risk: legible via per-territory Accord/Turmoil. What actors want: well-differentiated toolkits per faction. What happens if no one acts: mechanically explicit auto-Revolt/auto-Insurgency/GD-2 mandatory response.

degenerate_candidates: Total-hegemony-only endgame — GD-1 struck all alternates/Partition, so all factions converge to the identical terminal shape (15/15+Accord2+Turmoil≤6); 'myriad paths' delivered tactically, not in ending variety.

Insurgency doc-stated '11+ territory' win threshold (insurgency_pipeline_v30.md §7/§7.1) vs actual sim/canon 'all 15' — if ever implemented as written, a structurally easier win path for emergent factions than for established ones.

edges.mechanism: PT decay → Latent RM → Insurgency stages 1-2

directions.top-down: GD-1 philosophy (non-dominance) sits in tension with GD-1's own mechanic (single victory shape); the pressure system mitigates but doesn't resolve it.
directions.bottom-up: sim/autoload/victory.py correctly implements 'all 15,' contradicting insurgency_pipeline_v30's doc-level '11+' citation — sim is the trustworthy layer here.
directions.lateral: peninsular_strain_v30.md missed the GD-1 propagation pass that its sibling victory_v30.md received.
directions.diagonal: Battle→MS coupling ties the strategic layer directly to the Thread-crisis scale.
directions.forwards: GD-3 sim modules are named but several marked 'pending Pass 2l' in canon cross-references — verify landing before treating insurgency as implementation-ready.
directions.backwards: victory_v30.md still cites a stale open item ('pending Varfell Path B decision, ED-311') even though GD-1 already struck all Path B variants.

findings.claim: insurgency_pipeline_v30.md §7/§7.1 states Promoted Factions win at '11+ territories sustained 2 seasons,' contradicting GD-1's and victory_v30/peninsular_strain's 'all 15' and the actual sim (VICTORY_THRESHOLD=15 applied uniformly); same wrong figure recurs in canonical_sources.yaml.

peninsular_strain_v30.md §6.3 Peninsular Partition co-victory remains live, unmarked canonical text despite GD-1 explicitly striking Partition co-victory; sibling victory_v30.md §0.1 got the [SUPERSEDED-BY: GD-1] banner but peninsular_strain_v30.md did not, because GD-1's propagation cross-reference list never names this file.

MS is an unowned clock at the victory era-transition gates and world-state era transitions (Post-Calamity/Occupation/Anarchy) are unkeyed, meaning the Key/articulation stream is blind to the game's most consequential world-state shifts.
findings.tracked_ref: module_contracts.yaml victory gap_notes (charter calibration list: 'victory (unowned MS clock)')

findings.claim: GD-1 canon text names the threshold track 'Political Stability'; no design doc defines that name — it is Turmoil everywhere else, aliased only via an unlabeled sim comment.
victory_v30.md header still cites 'pending Varfell Path B user decision (ED-311)' as open, though GD-1 already struck all Southernmost Dominion/Path B variants.

GD-1's replacement of 8 faction-specific victories + Partition with one universal conquest endpoint narrows distinct victory shapes from ~9 to 1, in tension with the 'myriad options' North Star at the strategic-shape level even though tactical path variety is preserved.
